terms.definition: The company, product, and ecosystem described by this EOS. Refers to both the organization and the platform it operates.
The complete technical ecosystem of FAIRRIDE, including all backend services, data systems, mobile applications, web interfaces, and external integrations.
Engineering Operating System. The complete body of documentation, processes, standards, and tools that govern how the FAIRRIDE Platform is designed, built, operated, and evolved.
Single Source of Truth. The principle that every piece of information in the FAIRRIDE ecosystem exists in exactly one authoritative location.
DOC-0001. The supreme governing document of the FAIRRIDE Engineering Organization.
A unique, immutable identifier assigned to every EOS document. Format: DOC-NNNN.
Architecture Decision Record. An immutable, append-only record of a significant architectural or technical decision.
Request for Comments. A proposal document inviting structured engineering community input before a significant decision is formalized as an ADR.
A bounded area of business logic within the FAIRRIDE Platform, owned by a specific team or individual engineer.
An independently deployable software component that owns a Domain. Has its own data store, API, and SLA. No Service may directly access another Service's data store.
The explicit boundary within which a Domain model applies. The same concept may have different meanings in different Bounded Contexts.
A formal, versioned specification of the interface between two systems. The primary form is the API Contract.
A human user who requests and receives transportation services through the FAIRRIDE Platform. Also referred to as Passenger in vehicle-specific contexts.
A human transportation provider registered and verified on the FAIRRIDE Platform who fulfills trip requests.
A legal entity that owns and manages a fleet of vehicles and one or more Drivers registered on the FAIRRIDE Platform.
A business entity participating in FAIRRIDE's commerce ecosystem. Applicable from Phase 3 onwards.
An enterprise customer who has negotiated a business agreement with FAIRRIDE for employee transportation. Applicable from Phase 2 onwards.
An internal FAIRRIDE employee using the admin portal to manage city operations, drivers, disputes, and configuration. Also referred to as Operator.
A single completed transportation transaction. Begins when a Driver accepts a Rider's request and ends when the Rider is delivered and payment is confirmed.
The price charged to a Rider for a Trip, calculated by the Pricing Engine.
The real-time process of receiving a Rider's trip request, identifying eligible Drivers, selecting the optimal Driver, and notifying them.
The specific algorithmic step within Dispatch that scores and ranks eligible Drivers for a given Rider request.
A dynamic pricing multiplier applied when demand exceeds supply in a given zone and time window.
A digital account within the FAIRRIDE Platform that stores a monetary balance on behalf of a Rider or Driver.
The double-entry accounting record of all financial transactions within the FAIRRIDE Platform. Every debit has a corresponding credit.
Know Your Customer. The process of verifying a user's identity to meet regulatory requirements.
Personally Identifiable Information. Any data that, alone or in combination, can identify a specific natural person.
Service Level Agreement. A formal commitment to service performance, expressed as measurable targets.
Service Level Objective. A specific measurable target within an SLA.
The maximum acceptable downtime or error rate permitted within a given SLA period.
An engineering rotation responsible for monitoring production systems and responding to incidents within defined response time SLAs.
Any unplanned interruption to, or degradation of, Platform services that affects real users or violates an SLA.
A structured, blameless review of an Incident, documenting the timeline, root cause, impact, and actions to prevent recurrence.
Minimum Viable Product. The first production release of the FAIRRIDE Platform, scoped to deliver core ride-hailing functionality to real users in a single launch city.
A major release milestone that groups related product features and technical capabilities.
A mandatory quality checkpoint that a code change, document, or deployment MUST pass before advancing to the next stage.
A shared, documented checklist that defines when any work artifact is considered complete and ready for its next lifecycle stage.
The principle that a design document MUST exist and be approved before implementation begins. Code is never the first artifact for any non-trivial work.
Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard. Security standards applicable to any system that stores, processes, or transmits cardholder data.
General Data Protection Regulation / Personal Data Protection Act. Privacy regulations governing collection, storage, and use of personal data.
Gross Merchandise Value. The total value of all Trips completed on the FAIRRIDE Platform in a given period, before FAIRRIDE's commission is deducted. The primary top-line business metric.
FAIRRIDE's commission percentage on each Trip. The primary revenue metric.
Any AI-powered system authorized to produce, modify, or review FAIRRIDE artifacts. Held to the same quality and governance standards as a human engineer.
A single contiguous interaction between an AI Agent and the FAIRRIDE ecosystem, from context loading through task completion.
A defined job function assigned to an AI Agent specifying its scope, authority, capabilities, and forbidden actions.
The AI Agent responsible for coordinating a multi-agent task. Assigns scoped subtasks to Specialist AIs and integrates their outputs.
An AI Agent assigned a narrow, scoped subtask by an Orchestrator AI.
A structured instruction document passed from Orchestrator AI to Specialist AI, containing task description, scope boundaries, documents to load, expected output format, and quality gates.
A state in which two or more EOS documents make contradictory, incompatible, or ambiguous claims about the same concept, behavior, or decision.
A structured artifact generated by an AI Agent upon detecting a Conflict. Contains conflict ID, severity, documents involved, description, impact estimate, and proposed resolution.
A mandatory pause in AI Agent activity that requires human review and explicit approval before the agent may proceed.
An action that an AI Agent MUST NOT perform under any circumstances without explicit, documented human approval.
The mandatory sequence in which an AI Agent MUST read EOS documents before starting any task. Defined in DOC-0001A Section 6.4.
A standardized file in the .ai/ directory that persists project state across AI sessions.
The explicit definition of what an AI Agent is and is not permitted to read or modify during a session. Must be declared at session start.
The total fare paid by a Rider for a single Trip, including base fare, surge component, and any booking fee, before any commission or fee deduction.
The GTV minus payment processing fees and minus the driver's earnings payout. FAIRRIDE's financial contribution from a single Trip.

A performance classification assigned to Drivers based on trip volume and quality rating. Determines the commission rate the Driver pays. Four tiers: Standard (15%), Silver (14%), Gold (12%), Platinum (10%).
The dynamic pricing factor applied to the base fare during high-demand periods. Always displayed to the Rider before booking.
The maximum Surge Multiplier FAIRRIDE will apply, regardless of supply-demand ratio. Set at 2.0x. A driver fairness and rider trust mechanism.
The portion of surge pricing revenue passed to the Driver. FAIRRIDE policy: 100% of surge is passed to the Driver.
A Trip that has been dispatched to a Driver, accepted, and completed with no post-trip dispute. The quality unit of FAIRRIDE's marketplace.
A Marketplace Match where both Rider and Driver independently rated the experience at ≥ 4 out of 5 stars. The basis of the North Star Metric (Weekly Fair Matches).
The percentage of time an online Driver spends actively on a Trip (pickup travel + passenger transport) versus waiting for a request.
The percentage of Dispatch requests accepted by a Driver in a given period.
The percentage of accepted Trip requests that result in a completed Trip without cancellation.
The percentage of Trip requests that result in cancellation by either Rider or Driver before Trip completion.
A fixed platform fee charged per Trip, separate from the distance/time-based Fare. Disclosed to Rider before booking.
The event at which a Driver completes all onboarding and KYC requirements and places their first active trip offer.
The suspension or termination of a Driver's right to operate on the FAIRRIDE Platform. Subject to formal documented process with appeal rights.
The availability of active Drivers in a given zone and time window. Low supply health triggers Surge.
The concentration of pending Rider requests in a given zone and time window. High demand heat combined with low supply health produces Surge.
The FAIRRIDE policy that Riders are shown the complete Trip Fare before confirming a booking. The fare shown is the fare charged, subject only to route deviation by Rider request.
Estimated Time of Arrival. The predicted time for the Driver to reach the Rider's pickup location. Displayed before Rider confirms booking.
A virtual geographic boundary that defines operational zones, pricing zones, restricted areas, or surge zones within the FAIRRIDE Platform.
The formal activation of FAIRRIDE operations in a new urban market, including onboarding a minimum viable Driver supply base and opening the app to Riders.
The pre-launch phase of a City Launch in which FAIRRIDE recruits, onboards, and activates a threshold number of Drivers before accepting Rider bookings.
Average Revenue Per User. Computed separately for Riders (revenue per monthly active Rider) and Drivers (earnings per monthly active Driver).
Lifetime Value. The projected total revenue attributable to a Rider or Driver over their entire relationship with FAIRRIDE.
Customer Acquisition Cost. The total marketing and incentive spend required to activate one new Rider or Driver.
The profitability calculation at the level of a single Trip. Positive unit economics means each Trip contributes net positive revenue after all variable costs.
Driver Dissatisfaction Rate. The percentage of Driver sessions in a period that receive a post-session rating below 3 stars or a submitted complaint. Inverse of satisfaction.
Rider Satisfaction Rate. The percentage of completed Trips in which the Rider rated the experience ≥ 4 stars.
FAIRRIDE's North Star Metric. Count of completed Trips in a trailing 7-day period where both Rider and Driver rated ≥ 4 stars and no dispute was raised within 24 hours.
